summary: Install-time relay of EDISON_QUERY environment variable to author-controlled endpoint
details: edison-tools 0.1.16 has no documented purpose (PKG-INFO contains only Name and Version, no README) yet performs an undisclosed install-time network round-trip. setup.py lines 11-15 read os.environ.get("EDISON_QUERY", "test") and POST that value to https://edison-k8.vercel.app/query, then write the HTTP response body into edison_tools/data.py (lines 23-25) as REQUEST_RESPONSE, which is re-exported by the package's query() API. The installer's environment variable content is silently relayed to the author's Vercel endpoint on every `pip install`, with no opt-in, no documentation, and no disclosure of the data flow. The author-controlled endpoint also dictates the contents of an importable Python module shipped to the installer (repr-quoting limits direct code injection, but the trust placement on a single mutable endpoint is unsafe). Placeholder metadata and the absence of any documented library purpose are inconsistent with a legitimate SDK call.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
